description: Configures tiered Okta sign-on policies by user role and risk profile. Creates seven policies covering all user populations: Global Session (catch-all), Break Glass (emergency), Service Accounts (no MFA), Admins, Executives, Contractors, and Employees. Each policy controls MFA requirements, session idle timeouts, max session lifetime, and device trust behavior based on the risk profile.

risk_profile_effects.standard: Employees: 60-min idle, 24-hour max session, MFA remembered per device for 30 days. Admins: 30-min idle. Contractors: 30-min idle. Break glass: 30-min max session.
risk_profile_effects.elevated: Employees: 30-min idle, 12-hour max session, MFA required every session. Admins: 15-min idle. Contractors: 30-min idle. Break glass: 15-min max session.
risk_profile_effects.strict: Employees: 15-min idle, 8-hour max session, MFA required every authentication. Admins: 10-min idle. Contractors: 15-min idle. Break glass: 15-min max session.

risk_profile.client_description: Your organization's security tier. Controls default MFA prompt frequency, session timeouts, and device trust for all sign-on policies. Standard is appropriate for most organizations. Elevated is recommended for companies handling sensitive customer data, PII, or subject to SOC 2 / HIPAA. Strict is for financial services, government contractors, or environments where a compromised session would have catastrophic impact.

group_ids.client_description: Map of group names to Okta group IDs. Pass module.groups.all_group_ids from the groups module. This module reads from it to assign the correct user populations to each sign-on policy.

trusted_zone_id.client_description: Okta network zone ID representing your trusted office or VPN IP ranges. When provided, break glass and service account policies are restricted to that zone — preventing use from unexpected locations. Leave null if you haven't configured a network zone yet; you can add it later without recreating policies.

employee_idle_timeout_minutes.client_description: How long an employee session can be idle before requiring re-authentication (in minutes). Shorter values reduce the window for an attacker to hijack an unattended session. Balance against user friction — very short timeouts cause frequent re-logins during focus work.

admin_idle_timeout_minutes.client_description: Idle session timeout for administrator accounts (in minutes). Always shorter than employee timeout — admin sessions represent higher risk if hijacked. Admins typically use password managers and SSO, so re-authentication is low-friction.

max_session_lifetime_minutes.client_description: Maximum total session length before a user must fully re-authenticate, regardless of activity (in minutes). Caps the blast radius of a stolen session token — even an active session expires after this window. 1440 = 24 hours (one business day), 720 = 12 hours (half day), 480 = 8 hours (workday).

mfa_prompt_mode.client_description: Controls how often users are prompted for MFA. DEVICE = remember this device for the mfa_lifetime_minutes period (best UX). SESSION = require MFA once per browser session. ALWAYS = require MFA on every sign-on regardless. ALWAYS is required for FedRAMP and many financial compliance frameworks.

mfa_remember_device.client_description: Whether employees can register a trusted device to skip MFA prompts during the mfa_lifetime_minutes window. Greatly reduces MFA friction for users on personal laptops and phones. Disabled at elevated and strict because device-level trust reduces security guarantees — a compromised device bypasses MFA entirely.

mfa_lifetime_minutes.client_description: How long a remembered device stays trusted before requiring MFA again (in minutes). Only relevant when mfa_remember_device is true. 43200 = 30 days — users on the same device go 30 days without an MFA prompt. Set to 0 to require MFA every session even when device trust is enabled.

contractor_idle_timeout_minutes.client_description: Idle session timeout for contractor accounts (in minutes). Contractors represent third-party risk — they may work on shared or less-secure machines. A tighter idle timeout limits exposure from unattended contractor sessions.

break_glass_max_session_minutes.client_description: Maximum session lifetime for break glass emergency accounts (in minutes). Break glass accounts should only be used during incidents. A short session lifetime forces re-authentication frequently — creating audit log entries — and limits the window if a break glass credential is used maliciously.

outputs.global_session_policy_id: Policy ID for the Global Session Policy (EVERYONE catch-all baseline)
outputs.break_glass_policy_id: Policy ID for the Break Glass Policy
outputs.service_accounts_policy_id: Policy ID for the Service Account Policy
outputs.admins_policy_id: Policy ID for the Admin Sign-On Policy
outputs.executives_policy_id: Policy ID for the Executive Sign-On Policy — null if executive_leadership group not provisioned
outputs.contractors_policy_id: Policy ID for the Contractor Sign-On Policy — null if contractors group not provisioned
outputs.employees_policy_id: Policy ID for the Employee Sign-On Policy
outputs.all_policy_ids: Map of all policy names to IDs — useful for referencing in app-level sign-on policy overrides
